title: Study of RAS(ON) Inhibitors in Patients With Advanced RAS-mutated NSCLC

eligibility.justification: Age is a standard feasibility criterion for clinical trials.
The ECOG performance status reflects the patient's ability to perform daily activities, which can impact treatment response and thus the study's statistical power.

eligibility.text: Adequate organ function as outlined by the study
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have sufficient organ function to tolerate the trial's interventions and procedures, which is crucial for data quality.

eligibility.text: Received prior standard therapy appropriate for tumor type and stage
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have received standard treatment before entering the trial to avoid confounding factors that could affect the study's results.

eligibility.text: Must have pathologically documented, locally advanced or metastatic KRAS G12C-mutated solid tumor malignancy (not amenable to curative surgery)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is homogeneous and allows for a direct comparison of treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Must have pathologically documented, locally advanced or metastatic RAS-mutated NSCLC (Subprotocol B)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is homogeneous and allows for a direct comparison of treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Must have pathologically documented, locally advanced or metastatic RAS G12D-mutated NSCLC (Subprotocol C and Subprotocol D)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is homogeneous and allows for a direct comparison of treatment effects.
This criterion prioritizes patient safety by excluding individuals with CNS tumors, which are known to be particularly sensitive to treatment and may pose a risk of complications.

eligibility.text: Impaired gastrointestinal (GI) function that may significantly alter the absorption of RMC drugs
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have adequate GI function to absorb the study drug, which is crucial for data quality and treatment efficacy.

eligibility.text: Major surgery < 28 days of first dose
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have sufficient recovery time after surgery before starting the trial, which is crucial for data quality and treatment efficacy.

eligibility.text: Active or history of interstitial lung disease (ILD) or pneumonitis requiring steroids
eligibility.justification: This criterion prioritizes patient safety by excluding individuals with ILD or pneumonitis, which are known to be particularly sensitive to treatment and may pose a risk of complications.
This criterion acknowledges that additional criteria might be necessary for the study's success, which is crucial for data quality and treatment efficacy.

power_analysis.assessment: N/A — Phase 1 trial, not powered for efficacy

adaptive_designs.description: Adaptive design detected: Types identified: Dose-Finding / Phase I.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)
